raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2024, I noticed an unauthorized charge of {$310.00} on my account from a merchant named " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, '' categorized under Beauty and Spas. I did not authorize this transaction and promptly disputed it with Citibank. However, the dispute was declined with the reasoning that " your card has a chip in it, you must have used it. '' The decision to dismiss the case solely based on the use of a chip reader is insufficient, fails to address the possibility of fraudulent activity by the merchant, and is both unfair and incorrect. 

If Citibank insists that this transaction was authorized, I request that you provide clear and valid evidence supporting this claim, rather than simply stating your card has a chip in it. The merchant must be held accountable for providing proof, including a detailed receipt with my signature, to substantiate the charge. I reiterate that I do not recognize " XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX '' nor have I engaged in any transactions with this merchant. I do not know how they accessed my credit card information and how they were able to customize charges to post on my account. This incident constitutes credit card fraud, and I am the victim
